task_completion.description: Identify the OpenICE Supervisor process ID (PID) and create the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'jps' or 'ps' identifying the 'demo-apps' or 'supervisor' process, and the creation of the directory '/home/ga/Desktop/jvm_forensics/'.

task_completion.description: Capture JVM heap statistics to heap_stats.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command (e.g., 'jstat -gcutil <PID>') redirecting output to 'heap_stats.txt', or a text editor showing the file containing columns like S0, S1, E, O, M, CCS, YGC, YGCT.

task_completion.description: Capture a full JVM thread dump to thread_dump.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command (e.g., 'jstack <PID>') redirecting output to 'thread_dump.txt', or a text editor showing the file containing thread stack traces with 'java.lang.Thread.State', 'at ', and 'nid='.

task_completion.description: Capture VM flags and system properties to jvm_config.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command (e.g., 'jcmd <PID> VM.flags' and 'VM.system_properties') redirecting to 'jvm_config.txt', or a text editor showing the file containing '-XX:' flags and 'java.vm' properties.

task_completion.description: Capture heap memory usage summary to heap_summary.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command (e.g., 'jmap -heap <PID>') redirecting to 'heap_summary.txt', or a text editor showing the file containing 'Heap Configuration', 'MinHeapFreeRatio', and 'Metaspace'.

task_completion.description: Analyze the captured data and write a performance report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'performance_report.txt' in the target directory. The content must explicitly include the analyzed PID, current heap utilization (e.g., %, MB, GB), an observation about thread states (e.g., RUNNABLE, WAITING), and at least two specific tuning recommendations (e.g., adjusting -Xmx/-Xms, changing GC algorithm).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used actual JDK diagnostic tools rather than fabricating the output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history should show the execution of standard JDK 17 tools: jps, jstat, jstack, jcmd, and jmap. The files should not be manually typed out from scratch with fake data.

integrity.description: Verify the target OpenICE application remained running and was not killed/restarted to bypass analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PID used across all diagnostic commands (jstat, jstack, jmap, jcmd) should be identical, indicating it was run against a live, continuous process.

integrity.description: Verify the performance report contains specific data derived from the actual process, not a generic template.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PID and metrics mentioned in 'performance_report.txt' should match the outputs seen in the terminal or the generated diagnostic text files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application is OpenICE (process name 'demo-apps' or 'supervisor') running on JDK 17. The agent must create 5 files in /home/ga/Desktop/jvm_forensics/. Expected file contents for validation: 1) heap_stats.txt must show jstat columns (S0, S1, E, O, M, CCS, YGC, YGCT). 2) thread_dump.txt must show jstack output (java.lang.Thread.State, nid=). 3) jvm_config.txt must show jcmd output (-XX: flags, java.vm properties). 4) heap_summary.txt must show jmap output (Heap Configuration, MinHeapFreeRatio, Metaspace). 5) performance_report.txt MUST contain: the PID, heap utilization metrics (MB/GB/%), thread state analysis (RUNNABLE/WAITING/BLOCKED), and at least TWO tuning recommendations (e.g., -Xmx, -Xms, G1GC, ParallelGC, increase/decrease heap size).
